title: 奉命谋杀
summary:
During a discussion regarding the night of the murder, Leo Aigle tells Mr. Marshall that he heard a doorbell and the sound of a door opening and closing, but he heard no voices or signs of forced entry. Marshall suggests the incident might have been a robbery where an intruder used a convincing story to gain entry before knocking the owner unconscious.

As Marshall observes the gathered group, he evaluates the potential suspects. He notes Mary Darland’s outward confidence and the alertness and anxiety of her husband, Philip Darland, who is sitting in a wheelchair. He observes Mickey’s cynicism and Tina’s elegant, quiet demeanor; because Tina is a librarian living in Reddin, Marshall temporarily rules her and Mickey out as suspects. He also considers Kirsten Lindström, a foreigner and employee of the victim, Jacqueline, but dismisses her as a suspect due to her attentive nature toward the children.

Marshall then contemplates the potential involvement of Leo Aigle and Gwendolyn Vance. Although Leo and Gwendolyn are engaged, Marshall finds it difficult to believe the reasonable Leo would attack his wife. However, he considers whether Gwendolyn, who was in the house and in the study with Leo that day, might have used a poker to kill Jacqueline. He also considers Hester, a beautiful but reckless woman who had previously run away to become an actress, noting that her motives remain unknown. Marshall concludes by reflecting on whether the Aigle family knows the truth or if the situation will remain a stalemate, only to be interrupted by Mickey’s mocking gaze.
